summary: Iter45 backend testing of hosting quota enforcement on Exchange publish flow + APScheduler janitor (expire_lapsed_subscriptions). All 9 iter45 enforcement/janitor tests pass + 54/54 regression tests (iter42 webhooks/onboarding, iter43 runtime, iter44 hosting part3) remain green.
p0_coverage_verified: PUT /api/exchange/listings/{id} status=published with no sub -> 402 detail.error=NO_HOSTING_PLAN + upgrade_url=/hosting (freeuser)
Starter sub cap=1, used=0: publish 200 + agents_used=1 + listing_id in agents_published
Second publish hits cap -> 403 detail.error=agent_cap, max_agents=1; second listing remains draft
DELETE published listing decrements agents_used by 1, pulls listing_id from agents_published; second DELETE 404 (no negative)
PUT status=draft on published listing releases slot (agents_used -= 1)
Expired sub treated as no-subscription -> publish 402 NO_HOSTING_PLAN
expire_lapsed_subscriptions: cancelled past period_end -> expired (with expired_at); active past period_end -> expired (defensive); active w/ future period_end untouched
decrement_agents idempotent: second call on same listing_id does not push below floor
Admin (role=admin) bypasses cap; counter still bumps to track activity
Scheduler job id='hosting_expire' interval=hours=1 registered in /app/backend/server.py line 2121 (verified via grep of scheduler.add_job)
critical_code_review_comments: hosting.py:441 expire_lapsed_subscriptions is correct but does not skip rows already status='expired' explicitly - relies on status filter for cancelled/active. Safe but worth noting.

exchange.py:455 in upload_media auto-promote branch: _enforce_publish_quota is called BEFORE setting status=published. If quota check raises, the uploaded media file is already saved on disk but listing remains draft - the file is retained which is acceptable but means orphan media on quota-blocked uploads. Minor.

exchange.py:359 DELETE path correctly releases quota before delete - good. The 404 path (already-deleted listing) returns 404 from initial find_one without touching counter - matches idempotency requirement.
_release_publish_quota swallows ALL exceptions silently (line 86) which is intentional but could mask DB issues. Consider logging.
seed_data_creation: Tests self-seed hosting_subscriptions via _seed_active_sub() and clean up via _clear_subs() in finally blocks. Post-run DB inspection confirmed zero leftover rows for freeuser@test.com.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter45 hosting enforcement + janitor fully green. Scheduler job 'hosting_expire' confirmed registered. No frontend changes in this iteration. Mongo schema: hosting_subscriptions.status now supports 'expired' with expired_at timestamp; exchange_listings unchanged.
